ranked_discoveries.finding: Anti-regression is UNIVERSAL across ALL 7 therapeutic areas (mean rho=0.964)
Within-class spreads exceed between-class: 42 classes, mean 17pp, up to 90pp (antipsychotics)
Reporter sex UNCORRELATED with signal direction (r=-0.007), 53% paradoxical
Gold tier (highest confidence) is 72%F — stronger evidence = stronger female bias
Pan-therapeutic spectrum: CGRP 83%F to S1P 15%F (68pp range)
Male-biased signals STRONGER than female (|LR| 1.10 vs 1.06, p=3e-11)
GLP-1 agonists trending UP in female bias (r=0.76): 57%F (2005) → 65%F (2022)
AE archetypes: fibromyalgia 86%F, IBS 81%F, alopecia 76%F universally female across all drugs
HER2 (80%F) most female-biased drug target, VEGFR2 (42%F) most male-biased
Drug pair divergence: beta-blockers 21pp mean, 42% direction flip rate for shared AEs
Autoimmune drug concordance clusters (hydroxychloroquine↔leflunomide r=0.94)
Leuprorelin paradox: endometriosis drug shows male-biased signals (prostate cancer use)
ED drugs (sildenafil/tadalafil) 56%F — paradoxically female-biased due to PAH indication
CDK4/6 inhibitors 94%F — highest emerging drug class bias (breast cancer)
Corticosteroid metabolic AEs 76%F but infection AEs 53%F — organ-specific sex patterns
DOACs most uniform class (3pp spread) — safest for sex-balanced prescribing
Cannabinoids uniquely male-leaning (44%F) among pain drugs
Anti-infective allergy 71%F vs nephrotoxicity 45%F — organ drives sex bias
S1P modulators trending down significantly (r=-0.999, p=0.026)
Risperidone gynecomastia 0.1%F — most extreme male-biased high-volume signal

Endocrine.key: GLP-1 trending up, osteoporosis 73-92%F, statins 52%F balanced
